payload.text: Build a Jarvis voice assistant web application for mobile

**Defining Core Features**

I'm focused on outlining the essential features for this "Jarvis" web app. It needs voice interaction via the Gemini Live API and visual feedback that embodies a futuristic, "arc reactor" aesthetic. Real-time interaction with the Gemini 2.5 Flash Native Audio is a must, and it needs to be fully optimized for mobile use. I'm prioritizing these aspects for a solid foundation.


**Refining Initial Design**

I'm now diving into the design phase, starting with a Tailwind CSS foundation for a responsive and accessible user interface. I'm brainstorming features, potentially including simulated smart home controls and emphasizing helpfulness. The plan is to create metadata ("Jarvis AI"), styles (dark theme, neon blues), and key components (ArcReactor, VoiceInterface, Controls, Transcription). The `geminiLiveService.ts` is central to it all. The mobile UI is top priority.

entries.description: App metadata with required permissions for microphone.
Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS and viewport settings.
Shared types and interfaces for the application.
Helper functions for audio encoding and decoding.
A futuristic animated SVG component representing the Jarvis AI core.
Main application component managing UI state and Gemini Live interaction.

payload.text: Implement real-time, word-by-word transcription highlighting as the audio is processed to improve user feedback.

**Analyzing Current Implementation**

I'm currently looking into how the Jarvis voice assistant app handles transcriptions. Right now, it's using the `Gemini 2.5 Native Audio (Live API)` through `ai.live.connect`. It receives transcription chunks as both `inputTranscription` and `outputTranscription`. My next step is to understand how these chunks can be leveraged for real-time highlighting.


**Refining Text Chunk Handling**

I'm now focusing on how the app currently handles transcriptions by appending to `currentInput` and `currentOutput`, which are then moved to the `transcriptions` history upon receiving a `turnComplete` message. To achieve real-time word-by-word highlighting, I plan to refine the incoming text chunk handling for immediate feedback and implement a dynamic "typewriter" effect on `currentOutput` to feel reactive. I will also be updating `App.tsx` to manage partial transcriptions.


**Integrating New Features**

I realize that my prior focus on real-time highlighting now needs adjustment. The prompt expands the scope considerably, introducing new feature blocks. I have a lot more to cover, and my priority shifts towards integrating these new functionalities, including fast AI responses, video understanding, and image analysis, alongside refining the Live API. I'm taking notes.


**Re-evaluating Integration**

I'm now re-evaluating how to integrate the image editing capabilities using `gemini-2.5-flash-image` while prioritizing the core request for real-time word-by-word transcription highlighting. My concern is balancing the feature block with the focused instruction. I see the prompt structure now as a set of features and then the focused instructions. I'm focusing on "You MUST" sections, with the user's focus on the final section to guide the main work. I will be updating `App.tsx`.


**Prioritizing Core Features**

I'm now zeroing in on the immediate tasks. My plan is to update `App.tsx` to handle multimodal inputs, the image generation UI, and the grounding toggles, which include the word-by-word highlighting of transcriptions. Next, I'll update `metadata.json` for camera integration and will implement a processing visualizer in either `ArcReactor.tsx` or a new component. I will focus on the highlighting, as I think it's the core.


**Defining UI Architecture**

I'm now outlining a new UI, including a Live mode, similar to the Arc Reactor, a terminal, and a tools panel, but I will integrate the interface to keep it sleek. A "plus" button for attachments will be added, as well as a settings gear. Regarding word-by-word highlighting, I plan to render `currentOutput` as a component to highlight the most recent chunks. I'm focusing on the "MUST" requirements, and I've reviewed the specific models for features like Image Gen, Search, Maps, and Live functionality. I will also incorporate `gemini-2.5-flash-native-audio-preview-12-2025` for Live API.


**Defining Feature Integrations**

I'm now integrating new features, focusing on the UI overhaul of `App.tsx`. I will implement a "Media Upload" button, a "Mode Switcher" or intelligent prompt-based routing, and a "Command Center" UI to generate images. My focus is the `App.tsx` overhaul. I will also make updates such as the visual appearance of the Central Arc Reactor, minimal "floating controls", an attachment tray, Live Transcription with glow highlight, and a generation gallery.


**Implementing Core Features**

I'm now prioritizing the critical functional updates. Specifically, I plan to leverage `generateContent` for video and image analysis and focus on implementing a word-by-word renderer for the transcription text. I've realized `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` requires `openSelectKey()`, which I must address. I'm focusing on the spec, specifically the UI/UX features, and will make progress on the functional updates.

entries.description: Update metadata with camera permission for video understanding and image uploads.
Add support for a smoother futuristic font and styles for the word-by-word highlighting effect.
Overhaul App.tsx to include multi-model support, grounding tools, image generation interface, and word-by-word transcription highlighting.

payload.text:
Jarvis has been upgraded with the requested features:
1.  **Word-by-word Highlighting**: Transcriptions now feature a smooth, scale-and-glow effect that highlights the latest words received from the Live API.
2.  **Multimodal Analysis**: Added a "Plus" button to upload images or videos, which are then analyzed by **Gemini 3 Pro Preview**.
3.  **Image Generation**: Integrated **Gemini 3 Pro Image Preview** with a dedicated settings panel for aspect ratios (1:1, 9:16, 21:9, etc.) and resolution (1K to 4K).
4.  **Grounding Tools**: Added floating toggles for **Google Search** (using Gemini 3 Flash Preview) and **Google Maps** (using Gemini 2.5 Flash) to ensure JARVIS has the latest real-world data.
5.  **Futuristic UI**: The interface has been refined with a glass-morphism aesthetic, monospace data logs, and a more reactive Arc Reactor experience.
Add a dedicated section in the settings where users can view and edit JARVIS's system prompt to customize its personality and behavior.

**Considering System Prompt Editing**

I'm currently focused on the feasibility of letting users edit JARVIS's system prompt. My plan is to start by adding a `systemPrompt` state variable in `App.tsx`. This variable will hold the current system instruction, offering a configurable baseline for JARVIS's responses, offering potential user customization of JARVIS's personality. I'll need to figure out how to best integrate a settings section.


**Refining The Implementation**

I'm now refining the implementation plan. I'm focusing on UI integration within the existing settings panel. My idea is to append a "Core Personality" section, keeping the current mobile feel, rather than adding a new tab. I've confirmed that the `connect` function is using `sessionPromise`, which is good. My next step is to get the textarea and "Save" button working within the settings UI.


**Evaluating System Reactivity**

I'm now checking system reactivity. The Live API doesn't easily support mid-session system instruction changes, so I will ensure the `systemPrompt` updates when the session restarts. I'm focusing on making the state reactive. I'll add the `systemPrompt` state and the default system instruction to `App.tsx`. I will also integrate the textarea in the settings UI. Finally, I will ensure the user can easily scroll through settings.

entries.description: Add a personality customization section to the settings panel, allowing users to edit JARVIS's system prompt. Updated the connect logic to use the custom prompt.

payload.text: Add a text input field at the bottom of the screen, near the microphone button, allowing users to type their queries to JARVIS.

**Initiating Feature Prioritization**

I'm currently focused on prioritizing feature implementation for the JARVIS web application. The first step involves incorporating a "Thinking Mode" triggered by complex queries, using `gemini-3-pro-preview` with a `thinkingBudget` of 32768. I'm also planning to integrate image generation capabilities as a core feature.


**Expanding Feature Scope**

I'm now expanding the feature set for the JARVIS application. I will incorporate image generation with configurable aspect ratios, a standard chat interface using `gemini-3-pro-preview`, and video generation using `veo-3.1-fast-generate-preview` and specific aspect ratios. I'll also add image analysis capabilities and a text input field for more direct query entry. The current UI layout will be refined to accommodate all features.


**Refining User Interface**

I'm currently refining the user interface to integrate the new "Thinking Mode" and video generation features. I've added a "Thinking" toggle and clarified the `App.tsx` structure, with a new footer containing the text input field and action buttons. Settings will now house personality, image config (aspect ratios and size), video config (aspect ratio), and the thinking mode toggle.

entries.description: Add thinking mode (Gemini 3 Pro), video generation (Veo 3), and text input interface with updated settings and multimodal capabilities.

payload.text: Implement speech synthesis for JARVIS's responses, using the 'text-to-speech' functionality from the Gemini Live API. Allow users to customize voice options in settings.

**Considering Speech Synthesis**

I'm currently focused on integrating Gemini Live's text-to-speech capabilities into JARVIS's responses. I'm exploring how to seamlessly incorporate this functionality and how to create user-friendly voice customization options within the settings.


**Clarifying Text-to-Speech Implementation**

I'm now clarifying the TTS implementation. The Live API already handles audio natively, which simplifies the integration. The focus is now on enhancing user customization. I'll add voice selection options in the settings, specifically referencing available Live API voices: Puck, Charon, Kore, Fenrir, and Zephyr. I'm confirming JARVIS's speech uses the Live API for consistent integration.


**Expanding Speech Generation**

I'm now expanding the speech generation capabilities. Initially, I will update settings to include a "Voice Selection" dropdown, and update the Live API connection to use the selected voice. Secondly, text-based inputs will trigger an audio response using the `gemini-2.5-flash-preview-tts` model for consistency. I must incorporate a fallback for the text-only response.

entries.description: Add voice selection to settings and implement speech synthesis (TTS) for text-based responses to ensure JARVIS always speaks. Users can now choose from multiple prebuilt voices.

payload.text: Logo theme change in cool logo set

**Enhancing The App's Logo**

I'm thinking about the app's logo and current theme, specifically how to make them even "cooler." The existing `ArcReactor.tsx` component is already on a good track, giving off an Iron Man vibe, but I'm aiming for something more sophisticated. I am imagining a logo that feels more like "J.A.R.V.I.S." – sleek and intelligent.

entries.description: Overhaul the Arc Reactor logo with a more complex, multi-layered futuristic design featuring concentric rotating rings, data-dash patterns, and enhanced glow effects.
Refine the header logo text styling with futuristic spacing and a cyan neon effect to match the new Arc Reactor theme.

payload.text:
**Assessing Requirements**

I'm currently cross-referencing the initial prompt with the user's needs to ensure alignment. My focus is on thoroughly understanding all stipulated features and parameters, particularly the use of `gemini-3-pro-preview` with `thinkingBudget: 32768` within the web app's architecture. I am now evaluating the impact of these requirements.


**Defining Feature Implementation**

I've outlined feature implementation, prioritizing the core functions. The plan includes using specific Gemini models: `gemini-3-pro-image-preview`, `gemini-3-pro-preview`, `veo-3.1-fast-generate-preview`, and the Live API for voice interaction. Custom system prompts and an Arc Reactor theme are also incorporated, and I'm designing the Mobile UI with the text input near the mic. I'm focusing on the integration of these modules.


**Visualizing Interface Components**

I'm now zeroing in on UI details. The goal is a dark, sleek aesthetic with cyan accents, like Tony Stark's tech. I'm focusing on the Arc Reactor interaction point, glass-morphism panels, and the Orbitron font. I am working on a mobile-first responsive design, and defining how features will be represented and triggered within this design.

entries.description: Consolidated JARVIS experience with Live API, Deep Thinking (Gemini 3 Pro), Image/Video generation, media analysis, and custom system personality.

payload.text: Implement camera access for JARVIS to analyze images directly, adding a 'Analyze with Camera' button.

**Initiating Camera Integration**

I'm working on enabling camera access for image analysis within JARVIS. I've confirmed the `camera` permission is present in `metadata.json`. Next, I'm focusing on adding a "Analyze with Camera" button to the UI to trigger this functionality.


**Refining Camera Workflow**

I'm now implementing the camera capture sequence, focusing on getting a live preview. I've switched to `gemini-3-pro-preview` for image analysis. I'm adding a `VideoPreview` component within `App.tsx` and creating functions like `openCamera`, `captureAndAnalyze`, and `closeCamera`. I'm using `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia` for the camera stream, and adding a shutter button.


**Streamlining Capture Logic**

I'm now integrating the camera icon into the UI. I have the SVG defined. My strategy is to add a camera view overlay with shutter functionality triggered by the "Analyze with Camera" button. The flow starts with requesting camera access, followed by a preview. I'm focusing on stopping the feed and starting image analysis. The ultimate goal is to analyze images directly.


**Implementing Camera View**

I'm adding a `CameraView` section to `App.tsx` and incorporating state for camera activity. I've written `startCamera` to get a camera stream and set it to a video element. I'm storing the stream using a `useRef` to `cameraStreamRef`, and will use it to handle stopping. I have a plan for `captureImage` too, capturing and encoding the data to a base64 string.


**Refactoring Image Analysis**

I'm now refactoring the `analyzeMedia` function. My focus is streamlining image analysis, and I've identified code duplication that can be addressed by accepting base64 data directly. I'll make a helper function, `analyzeBase64`, to handle this, as the current `analyzeMedia` function accepts a `File` object.

entries.description: Add direct camera capture and analysis functionality. Includes a new camera button, video preview overlay, and logic to capture frames and send them to Gemini 3 Pro for analysis.

payload.text: When JARVIS is actively listening, add a subtle visual indicator or animation around the Arc Reactor to show that it's processing audio input. This will provide better user feedback.

**Considering Image Integration**

I'm currently focused on how to best integrate image editing capabilities, specifically using the `gemini-2.5-flash-image` model (Nano Banana). I'm thinking about user-friendly text prompts such as "Apply a retro filter" or "Eliminate the background figure." Additionally, I intend to implement a visual cue around the Arc Reactor.


**Implementing Image Editing**

I've outlined specific specifications for the image editing feature, including gallery integration and the use of text prompts. The plan is to trigger the `gemini-2.5-flash-image` model through `generateContent`, and capture the output. I'm also planning to enhance the visual feedback around the Arc Reactor, making it pulse or ring to more clearly indicate the "listening" state when connected.

entries.description: Add image editing functionality using Gemini 2.5 Flash Image and enhance the Arc Reactor's visual feedback during active listening.
